topic: Context 2: Local, National, International and Global Areas of Interest
subject: french

items.q: How do you say 'There is a cinema in my town'?
items.correct: Il y a un cinéma dans ma ville
items.misconception: Il a un cinéma ma ville
items.why: Students misuse 'il y a' and gender; it is 'il y a un cinéma', with the correct article.

items.q: What does 'les transports en commun' mean?
items.why: Students mistranslate it; 'en commun' means shared/public transport, relevant to local and global travel.
Students confuse personal topics with global ones; climate change is a worldwide area of interest.
It's Poverty. “Wealth” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
